title: What School Absences Really Reveal About Students
subtitle: 缺课背后的深层信号

paragraphs.en: Nationally, one in six children miss 15 or more days of school in a year. Education officials have deplored all this missed instruction. These chronically absent students suffer academically because of all the classroom instruction they miss out on.
paragraphs.zh: 在全美范围内，六分之一的儿童每年缺课15天或以上。教育官员对大量教学时间的流失深感痛惜。这些长期缺勤的学生因错过课堂授课而在学业上受到严重影响。

paragraphs.en: In 2015, the US secretary of education responded to this crisis, urging communities to support every student to attend every day and be successful in school. His open letter stated that missing 10% of school days in a year for any reason — excused or unexcused — is a primary cause of low academic achievement. Worrying about whether children attend school makes sense. After all, if students don't show up, teachers can't teach them.
paragraphs.zh: 2015年，美国教育部长对这一危机作出回应，敦促社区支持每位学生每日到校并取得学业成功。他在公开信中指出，一年中无论因何种理由缺课达10%（无论获准与否），都是学业成绩低下的主因。担忧孩子是否上学合情合理，毕竟学生不到场，教师便无法施教。

words.meaning: adj. 获准的，有正当理由的

paragraphs.en: But what if America's attendance crisis is about much more than students missing class? What if, instead, it is a reflection of family and community crises these students face — such as being ejected from the family apartment, fearing for their safety in their neighborhood or suffering an illness? As social scientists we investigated how excused and unexcused absences relate to children's academic achievement.
paragraphs.zh: 但如果美国的出勤危机远不止学生缺课这么简单呢？如果它恰恰反映了这些学生面临的家庭与社区危机——比如被逐出公寓、担忧社区安全或身患疾病呢？作为社会科学家，我们研究了获准缺勤与无故缺勤如何关联儿童的学业成绩。

paragraphs.en: We find that absences excused by a parent do little to harm children's learning. In fact, children with no unexcused absences — but 15 to 18 excused absences — have test scores equal to their peers who have no absences. Meanwhile, the average child with even just one unexcused absence does much worse academically than peers with none.
paragraphs.zh: 我们发现，由家长请假的获准缺勤对儿童学习损害甚微。事实上，没有无故缺勤但获准缺勤15至18天的儿童，其考试成绩与从未缺勤的同龄人不相上下。而即便只有一次无故缺勤的普通儿童，学业表现也远逊于从未无故缺勤的同龄人。

paragraphs.en: We believe unexcused absence is a strong signal of the many challenges children and families face, including economic and medical hardships. Unexcused absences can be a powerful signal of how those out-of-school challenges affect children's academic progress. Our evidence suggests unexcused absences are problematic, but for a different reason than people often think.
paragraphs.zh: 我们认为，无故缺勤是儿童及家庭面临诸多挑战（包括经济和医疗困境）的强烈信号。无故缺勤有力地表明校外困境如何影响儿童的学业进步。我们的证据显示无故缺勤确有问题，但原因与人们通常所想的不同。

paragraphs.en: Absence from school, and especially unexcused absence, matters mainly as a signal of many crises children and their families may be facing. It matters less as a cause of lower student achievement due to missed instruction. How we choose to think of school absences matters for educational policy.
paragraphs.zh: 缺课，尤其是无故缺课，其重要性主要在于它是儿童及其家庭可能面临的诸多危机的信号。作为因错失教学而导致成绩下降的原因，它的重要性反而次之。我们如何看待缺课，对教育政策至关重要。

paragraphs.en: School attendance policies typically hold schools and families accountable for the days children miss, regardless of whether they were excused or unexcused absences. These policies assume that missing school for any reason harms children academically because they are missing classroom instruction. They also assume that schools will be able to effectively intervene by reducing student absences. We find neither to be the case.
paragraphs.zh: 出勤政策通常要求学校和家庭对孩子缺课天数负责，无论缺课是否获准。这些政策假定任何理由的缺课都会因错过教学而损害学业，也假定学校能通过减少缺勤进行有效干预。我们发现两者均不成立。

paragraphs.en: As a result, these attendance policies end up disproportionately punishing families dealing with out-of-school crises in their lives and pressuring schools who serve them to get students to school more often. We instead suggest using unexcused absence from school as a signal to channel resources to the children and families who need them most.
paragraphs.zh: 结果，这些出勤政策最终不成比例地惩罚了那些正应对校外生活危机的家庭，并向服务这些家庭的学校施压以提高出勤率。相反，我们建议将无故缺勤作为信号，将资源引向最需要帮助的儿童和家庭。
